title: [Sentry ERROR] DriveError: Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** DriveError: Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.
**Location:** Be(babybloom/assets/index-DvGuPSHc)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 3 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-04-01T07:20:46.317000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-04-01T07:23:01.890000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7379277222/
**Sentry ID:** 7379277222

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description:
**What was reported:**
Sentry captured a DriveError ('Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.') occurring 3 times between 07:20–07:23 UTC on 2026-04-01. The error originates in the minified bundle at function Be() in babybloom/assets/index-DvGuPSHc.

**Area affected:**
Google Drive sync feature in `src/` — specifically the OAuth scope configuration and/or the Drive API call that references a space (e.g., 'appDataFolder', 'drive', or 'drive.file') not covered by the scopes the user has granted. This is closely related to recent commit 6777566 which switched to drive.file scope + Google Picker.

**Root cause hypothesis:**
After switching to `drive.file` scope, some code path still attempts to access a Drive space (e.g., `appDataFolder` or a general `drive` space) that `drive.file` does not cover. The Picker API flow may grant `drive.file` but a subsequent Drive API call may reference `appDataFolder` or list files outside the Picker-granted set.

**Expected behavior:**
The app should only request and use scopes consistent with the drive.file scope granted via Google Picker. Any API call to spaces not covered by drive.file should either be removed or guarded so it only runs when the appropriate scope is available.

**Current behavior:**
A DriveError is thrown at runtime because the requested Drive space is outside the granted scope, causing the sync operation to fail.

**Edge cases to handle:**
- User who previously granted broader scopes and revoked them
- User on first-time Picker auth flow vs. re-auth flow
- Any `appDataFolder` references that may still exist after the scope downgrade

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. No DriveError 'granted scopes do not give access' thrown in normal sync flow
2. All Drive API calls only reference spaces covered by `drive.file` scope
3. If a broader scope is needed, the code explicitly re-prompts the user rather than failing silently

**Testing:**
- Add unit test that mocks the Drive API and asserts no appDataFolder/unsupported-space calls are made when only drive.file scope is granted
- Add regression test: simulate the scope mismatch scenario and verify a graceful error (not a raw DriveError) is shown to the user
- Verify existing sync tests still pass after the fix
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed:
  src/lib/sync/googleDrive.ts — (1) Added hasRequiredDriveScope() helper that validates token scope contains drive.file or drive; (2) In getAccessToken(), added early scope validation throwing scope_insufficient when token has incompatible scope (e.g. drive.appdata only); (3) Added spaces: drive to all files.list queries (getOrCreateFolder search, listDeviceFiles, findFileId) to prevent implicit space inference errors; (4) In handleDriveError(), added detection of "granted scopes" / "requested spaces" in 403 messages, mapping to new scope_insufficient DriveErrorCode instead of generic forbidden; (5) Added scope_insufficient to DriveErrorCode union and its userMessage; (6) Added user-facing message: "Please re-connect Google Drive in Settings → Cloud Sync."
  src/lib/sync/syncEngine.ts — Added scope_insufficient to skipCodes set so this user-action-required condition is not reported to Sentry as a code bug.
- Tests added:
  tests/unit/driveScope.test.ts — 15 tests covering: scope_insufficient DriveErrorCode exists, handleDriveError scope detection, getAccessToken scope validation, hasRequiredDriveScope logic (drive.file and drive both accepted), no appDataFolder in API calls, spaces=drive in getOrCreateFolder/listDeviceFiles/findFileId, scope_insufficient excluded from Sentry.
- Approach taken:
  Root cause: After switching to drive.file scope, (1) files.list calls lacked explicit spaces=drive, allowing Google to infer spaces that drive.file may not cover, and (2) the 403 "granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces" error was mapped to forbidden instead of a specific actionable code. Users with stale tokens (wrong scope) had no clear path to fix the issue.
  Fix: Three-layered defense: token scope pre-validation (catch before API call), explicit spaces=drive on all list queries (prevent API-level inference errors), and specific error code mapping (turn opaque 403 into actionable scope_insufficient with clear user message).
- Trade-offs: Token scope validation only fires when tokens.scope is present (not all token stores record scope). This is intentional — tokens without a scope field are assumed valid to avoid breaking existing sessions.

title: [Sentry ERROR] DriveError: Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** DriveError: Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.
**Location:** Ao(babybloom/assets/index-DvGuPSHc)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 1 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-04-01T07:17:44.755000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-04-01T07:17:44.755000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7379272319/
**Sentry ID:** 7379272319

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description:
**What was reported:**
Sentry captured a DriveError ("Google Drive access denied: The granted scopes do not give access to all of the requested spaces.") — 1 event, 0 users affected. First seen 2026-04-01T07:17:44Z (slightly before #189 which was first seen at 07:20:46Z). The error originates in function Ao() in the minified bundle at babybloom/assets/index-DvGuPSHc — a different function than #189 (which was Be()).

**Relationship to #189:**
Issue #189 (same error, function Be()) has already been fixed (commit eae707d, closes #189). This issue is Sentry event 7379272319 — a distinct Sentry issue from #189's 7379277222 — suggesting the scope mismatch manifested in a different call path (Ao vs Be in the minified output).

**Area affected:**
Google Drive sync feature in src/lib/sync/googleDrive.ts — the same file fixed for #189. The fix for #189 added: hasRequiredDriveScope() validation, spaces: drive on all files.list queries, and scope_insufficient DriveErrorCode. However, function Ao() in the minified bundle may correspond to a different exported function or code branch not yet covered by those changes.

**Root cause hypothesis:**
After the #189 fix, a second code path in googleDrive.ts may still make Drive API calls without explicit spaces: drive or without the token scope pre-check. The minifier assigns different short names (Ao vs Be) to different functions, so this is a distinct entry point into the Drive API.

**Expected behavior:**
All Drive API interactions should be guarded by the scope validation added in #189. No DriveError for scope mismatch should reach the user or Sentry.

**Current behavior:**
A separate Sentry event (7379272319) fired from function Ao() — indicating a code path missed by the #189 fix still throws the raw scope error.

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Identify which function/exported method maps to Ao() in the minified output (likely a different public method in googleDrive.ts — e.g., uploadFile, downloadFile, deleteFile, or syncAll)
2. Apply the same three-layer defense as #189: token scope pre-validation, explicit spaces: drive on any files.list, and scope_insufficient error code mapping
3. Confirm Sentry event 7379272319 would not fire after the fix
4. All existing sync unit tests pass

**Testing:**
- Extend tests/unit/driveScope.test.ts (created in #189) to cover the Ao() code path
- Add a test for the specific method identified in step 1 above, asserting scope_insufficient is thrown (not a raw DriveError) when token scope is wrong
- Verify no regression in existing tests
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: tests/unit/driveScope.test.ts — extended with 14 new tests covering the Ao() code path
- Tests added: tests/unit/driveScope.test.ts covers uploadFile, downloadFile, deleteFile, getFileModifiedTime, findFileId, and handleDriveError 403 scope-error branch
- Approach taken: The #189 fix (commit eae707d) already applied all three defensive layers to googleDrive.ts — getAccessToken() scope pre-validation, spaces:drive on all files.list calls, and scope_insufficient error mapping in handleDriveError. Sentry event 7379272319 (#190) fired from function Ao() in the minified bundle, a different call chain (uploadFile/downloadFile/deleteFile → findFileId) than Be() which was the getOrCreateFolder path fixed in #189. Both paths are now guarded by the same defenses. This issue adds the missing test coverage to explicitly verify the Ao() call chain.
- No production code changes were needed — the #189 fix was comprehensive. Only tests added.
- Ao() path identified as: uploadFile/downloadFile/deleteFile → findFileId (each of which now has spaces:drive and getAccessToken() pre-validation)
